description_long: This is a single-story log cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in dark shingles, extending well beyond the walls to provide deep eaves. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontally stacked, rounded wooden logs with visible interlocking corners, giving it a traditional rustic appearance. The foundation appears to be made of stone or concrete, elevating the structure slightly above ground level. There are a few rectangular windows, mostly grouped together on one side, and a single door located at the end of the building, adjacent to a small covered porch area. The porch is defined by a simple wooden railing and posts, matching the log construction of the main structure. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for residential or recreational use in a natural or suburban setting.
description: A rustic log cabin with a dark gable roof, stone foundation, grouped windows, and a small covered porch at one end.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long log wall, grouped windows, and the edge of the porch area.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the gable roof, a single window, and the entrance door with porch railing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a continuous log wall and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: End view with the porch, entrance door, and a window, showing the log construction and roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with deep overhangs, covered in dark asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows grouped together on one long side, with a single window on the end near the entrance; arrangement is asymmetrical and clustered.
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located at the end of the cabin, adjacent to the porch area.
